question.text: Stamp duty on mutual fund purchases since July 2020 is:

answer.text: 0.1% on all MF transactions including redemptions
No stamp duty is applicable on mutual fund transactions
0.005% on all MF purchases, not applicable on redemptions

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Since July 1, 2020, stamp duty of 0.005% is levied on all mutual fund purchases (including SIPs, switches-in, and fresh purchases). It is NOT applicable on redemptions. The duty is deducted from the investment amount before unit allotment. **The Trap:** The rate is very small (0.005% = Rs.5 per Rs.1 lakh invested) and applies to purchases only. Many candidates forget that switch-in is also a purchase and attracts stamp duty. **Cross-Exam Context:** Stamp duty was introduced under the Indian Stamp Act amendments. RTAs collect and remit this duty as part of transaction processing. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** At 0.005%, stamp duty on a Rs.1 lakh investment is just Rs.5. It's negligible for most investors but adds up for large institutional transactions. Mention it for transparency but don't let it deter investment decisions.
